source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Deepwater Nautilus Structural Damage

event.top_event: Uncontrolled descent of traveling block and top drive
actions_taken: Emergency hydraulic dump activated by Assistant Driller
Equipment replacement and acceptance testing performed
Hydraulic solenoid valves inspected and replaced
event.summary: On June 9, 2014, while troubleshooting a brake fault alarm on the drawworks system during pipe transfer operations, the Chief E.T. performed a hard reset of drive #4 without confirming that the drawworks brakes were set. Due to miscommunication between the Toolpusher and Chief E.T., the reset interrupted motor control while the top drive was still being held up, causing the traveling block to freefall. The Assistant Driller activated the emergency hydraulic dump, which caused the brakes to slam shut but failed to prevent impact with the rig floor. Damage occurred to drill pipe, the Driller's shack, and the traveling assembly. Root cause analysis identified sticking hydraulic solenoid valves due to debris accumulation and improper hydraulic pump installation.
recommendations: Improve communication protocols between crew members during troubleshooting operations
Establish mandatory verification procedures before performing system resets
Implement enhanced maintenance procedures for hydraulic solenoid valves
Conduct training on proper identification and investigation of maintenance alarms before attempting corrective actions
key_phrases: Maintenance Torque up Time Out for Internal Brake Release

hazards.name: Suspended load (traveling block and top drive)

threats.name: Hydraulic solenoid valve sticking due to debris
Loss of motor control during hard reset
Inadequate troubleshooting procedure before reset attempt

consequences.name: Uncontrolled freefall of traveling block and top drive
Impact damage to drill pipe and rig floor equipment
Potential personnel injury from falling load

controls.name: Drawworks brake system
controls.lod_basis: Primary mechanical brake system designed to hold suspended load

supporting_text: When the Driller tried to set the brakes on the drawworks, he received a 'Maintenance Torque up Time Out for Internal Brake Release' fault alarm
The breaks shut, but due to the momentum for the top drive, failed to stop the traveling block from falling to the rig floor

controls.name: Fault alarm system for brake operations
controls.lod_basis: Automated system designed to stop all operations if brake fault alarm occurs

supporting_text: The system is designed to stop all operations if a brake fault alarm occurs during operations

controls.name: Emergency hydraulic dump (Fire-Axe Handle)
controls.lod_basis: Manual emergency system to dump hydraulic pressure and engage brakes

supporting_text: Upon seeing the drill pipe start to buckle towards the drill shack, the Assistant Driller pulled the 'Fire-Axe Handle', which is designed to dump the hydraulic pressure from the drawworks brake circuit in an emergency
The breaks shut, but due to the momentum for the top drive, failed to stop the traveling block from falling to the rig floor

controls.name: Troubleshooting procedure and verification protocol
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement to identify root cause before attempting corrective actions

supporting_text: Crew members failed to properly investigate maintenance alarms before attempting to reset the drawworks
The crew proceeded to reset the system without first identifying the cause of the alarm or ensuring that it was safe to do so

controls.name: Communication protocol between crew members
controls.barrier_role: Ensure accurate information exchange during critical operations
controls.lod_basis: Clear communication required to confirm system status before performing resets

supporting_text: The Toolpusher, who was now sitting in the Driller's chair, took the call and said something to the effect of 'the brakes are not set' at the same time an announcement was being made over the rigs intercom system. The Chief E.T. misunderstood and thought he heard 'brakes are set'
Poor communication between the crew members led to confusion and misleading information in all areas of the operation

controls.name: Hydraulic solenoid valve maintenance and inspection
controls.barrier_role: Prevent sticking of brake pilot pressure valves
controls.lod_basis: Regular maintenance to remove debris and ensure valve operability

supporting_text: The investigation that followed the third incident showed that the cause of the excessive heating was due to the crew improperly installing a hydraulic pump on the braking system
Reports from Stress Engineering Services concluded that 'the debris in the valves was of sufficient size and quantity to cause the sticking of these valves'
It was determined that the same situation had triggered the brake alarm during the first incident

controls.name: Personnel awareness and rig floor evacuation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Minimize personnel exposure to falling load hazard
controls.lod_basis: Immediate evacuation of rig floor when uncontrolled movement detected

supporting_text: The Driller, on his way to lunch, saw the movement and ordered all personnel to clear the rig floor
No injuries or pollution were reported after the incident

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
